idea-plugin.name: igni
idea-plugin.description:
igni is an AI coding assistant that lives in a chat tool window alongside your editor. It reads your project, runs multi-agent workflows in parallel (research, plan, implement, review), and drives real edits through the same tool surface you use — file edits, shell commands, semantic code search, MCP integrations — with an approval dialogue on every tool call.

What you can do

  • Chat with your codebase — @-mention files to attach them, run slash commands to drive structured actions, refactor across multiple files in one turn.
  • Plan mode — the agent researches your code and proposes a plan before touching anything. Approve as-is or refine.
  • Multi-agent orchestration — spawn parallel sub-agents so a large task decomposes into concurrent research + implementation + review, each streaming progress into the same chat.
  • Session per project — chat history persists per repository. Fork a session to explore an alternative approach without losing the original thread.
  • Cross-window sync — open the plugin in JetBrains and the VSCode extension side-by-side; both talk to the same backend for the project, so what you type in one appears live in the other.
  • Model-agnostic — Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, MiniMax, and any model reachable via API. Switch mid- session with /model.
  • Follows your IDE theme — Darcula, Light, High Contrast, or any custom look-and-feel. The chat panel re-paints the moment you switch themes in Preferences.

Zero-touch install

Nothing to install by hand. On first launch the plugin downloads uv and uses it to provision Python and ignite-ember into a per-user cache. From there, network use is whatever your configured model provider needs — Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.

Open via Tools → igni → Open Chat or ⌘E / Ctrl+E. Right-click any file or selection to attach it to the chat.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.9.5

  • Fixed the dark tool window in 2025.x IDEs. PyCharm / IntelliJ 2025.3 moved JCEF out-of-process (a shared cef_server sidecar), and our windowed-mode browser request was being silently dropped there — the chat panel mounted but never painted, leaving a solid dark rectangle. The plugin now detects remote-mode at runtime and falls back to OSR with the display's native refresh rate.
  • 120 Hz rendering enabled by default. On first launch we flip ide.browser.jcef.out-of-process.enabled to false so JCEF runs in-process and windowed browsers work at your display's refresh rate (120 Hz on ProMotion MacBooks). One-time, IDE-wide. To revert to the sandboxed default: Tools → igni → Disable 120 Hz Rendering (Restart).
  • Calmer chat panel in dark IDE themes. The send button was rendering the ember orange-to-red gradient in Darcula / New UI dark chrome, reading as "brand promo" rather than a primary action. It now uses a subtle raised chip that follows the IDE theme, with a thicker white arrow matching the mode-label weight. VSCode dark themes get the same treatment. Web, Tauri, and light themes keep the ember gradient.
  • Panels match IDE chrome. CodeIndex / MCP / Watcher / Plugins drawers now paint on the IDE's own Panel.background (JetBrains) or sideBar.background (VSCode) instead of a brand-brown card. Composer input, chips, and org tags keep their brand tones — only the outer canvas swaps.
  • Notification group renamed from "EmberCode" to "igni" in Preferences → Notifications so the entry matches the plugin's brand.

0.9.2

  • Shared backend per project. Open the plugin in JetBrains and the VSCode extension side-by-side on the same repo — both now talk to the same Python backend (via a discovery lockfile at <project>/.ember/backend.lock). What you type in one window streams live into the other's chat. Also halves memory use vs the old spawn-per-IDE behaviour.
  • Version-mismatch guard. If two clients on different plugin versions try to share a backend, the second one now surfaces a clear notification instead of silently corrupting state.
  • Fixed cross-view "Finalizing…" stuck state. When another view's run finished, this view could latch on the tail indicator forever. Rewrote the observer's busy-state reducer with a proper test harness pinning down the regression.
  • Session listing hardening. Canonicalised --project-dir so /tmp vs /private/tmp and unresolved symlinks converge on one DB. Removed the 20-session cap. Refused stale FE session_ids from other projects (the "why does the sidebar show a chat from a different repo" bug).

0.9.1

  • JetBrains plugin now runs at 120 fps. Switched JCEF to windowed mode — bypasses the JBR OSR paint pipeline that was AWT-gated at ~30 Hz on macOS. Scroll + streaming feels fluid instead of choppy.
  • Native IDE theme integration. The tool window background now sources from IntelliJ's Panel.background, so the chat reads as native IDE chrome under Darcula, Light, High Contrast, or any custom LAF — no more differently-shaded patch. Theme flips live when you switch in Preferences.
  • Removed the sun/moon picker inside the panel — the IDE owns light/dark now, so a second control was just fighting the LafManagerListener signal.
  • Dropped a 12-layer backdrop-filter blur ramp from the header + sidebar — it was the dominant per-frame cost on JCEF's software compositor. Flat panels look nearly identical and cost nothing.
  • Optional live FPS overlay via ⌘⌥⇧F (or Tools → igni → Toggle FPS Counter).

0.9.0

  • Backend version verification across all clients. A new "cli · vX.Y.Z" chip in the header shows the actual running ignite-ember version. Warn tone when a dev-override env is active; danger tone on hard mismatch between what the plugin expected and what the runtime reports.
  • Diagnose Backend action. Tools → igni → Diagnose Backend dumps plugin version, resolved CLI path, actual CLI version, and bootstrap env for one-click bug reports.
  • Managed-venv marker check catches the case where the cached CLI drifts from the pin and offers a clean reinstall.

0.8.3

  • HITL "Always allow" actually stops asking. After approving with "Always allow similar", the same tool call would still hit the permission prompt on the next invocation because the tool_hook chain re-blocked in the ASK branch after the evaluator flipped to ALLOW. Fixed at the hook layer.

0.8.2

  • HITL "Always allow" now persists across calls. The session's in-memory PermissionEvaluator was built once at startup and never re-read settings.local.json, so approvals written to disk were only picked up next launch. Also fixed a rule-string format mismatch between the persisted decisions and the evaluator's fnmatch.

0.8.1

  • Async tools now correctly declare requires_confirmation, so tools that historically slipped past the permission dialog (write, edit, save) are gated the same as sync ones.

0.8.0

  • Watcher panel. Live view of every background task the agent has running — long shells, watch loops, scheduled jobs — with per-item Stop.
  • Persisted plan decisions. Approve or dismiss a plan and the card stays in that state across reloads and session switches. No more "did I approve this?" re-approvals.
  • Split send button. Primary click sends; dropdown carries a "Send in plan mode" alternate action for a one-shot planned turn without flipping your session's default mode.

0.7.1 – 0.7.5

  • Release-pipeline hardening: several rounds of updater signature fixes for the Tauri desktop shell + PyPI metadata refresh. No user-facing plugin changes for JetBrains beyond the bundled ignite-ember bump. Users on any 0.7.x upgrade cleanly.

0.6.4

  • Auto-updater finally works on macOS. Required two missing pieces: bundle.createUpdaterArtifacts: true in tauri.conf.json so the .app.tar.gz + .sig actually get emitted, and a workflow step that stashes the .sig before tauri-action's upload pass cleans it up. Includes a minisign re-sign fallback if the .sig is gone by the time we reach for it. v0.6.0 / 0.6.1 / 0.6.2 / 0.6.3 clients will get a real update prompt for 0.6.4 — the first release with a valid latest.json attached.
  • Same product surface as 0.6.1 — no UI changes.

0.6.3

  • Re-attempt of the 0.6.2 updater fix. The previous workflow used actions/upload-artifact's glob to stash .app.tar.gz.sig from the macOS build dirs, but the glob silently matched zero files even though the file was at exactly that path, failing both macOS matrix legs. Switched to a find + cp step that copies the sig to a stable no-spaces path before the upload-artifact step picks it up.
  • Same set of features as 0.6.1 — frosted-glass header / sidebar, custom scroll indicators, faster post-stream tail, knowledge-index warmup, window-drag from the chat header, dark-tile app icon, generic DMG installer icon.

0.6.2

  • Release-pipeline fix: latest.json for the Tauri auto-updater is now assembled and uploaded by our own workflow step. tauri-action's built-in path was silently skipping with "Signature not found for the updater JSON" on Tauri 2 + product names with spaces, so previous releases shipped without the updater manifest and clients couldn't detect new versions. Users on 0.6.0 / 0.6.1 will receive an update prompt for 0.6.2.
  • No JetBrains-side changes in this release; bundled ignite-ember bumped to 0.6.2 in lockstep via build.gradle.kts.

0.6.1

  • Frosted-glass header + sidebar — messages scrolling behind the brand row and the "Sessions" cluster now fade through a six-layer progressive backdrop blur instead of being clipped at a hard edge. Custom scroll-position indicators paint above the blur so the thumb stays sharp.
  • Faster perceived response time — Agno's per-turn auto user-profile + session-context extractions are off by default. They were firing a separate LLM call after each run, adding 5–10 s between the visible answer ending and the stats line landing. Agentic memory tools still run on demand.
  • Knowledge index warms in the background at session start, so the first /knowledge press no longer waits on the Chroma client to open.
  • Window dragging from the chat header in the Tauri shell (was previously gated by a missing core:window:allow-start-dragging capability). New dark-tile app icon; downloaded .dmg no longer shows the app icon on the disk image.

0.6.0

  • Multi-agent orchestration — the chat panel now drives a session pool, so you can run multiple parallel conversations (one per project, or N per project) in the same IDE window. Each tool window gets its own session, with live typing + message mirroring across IDE / web / Tauri views bound to the same session.
  • Project-lock UX — header chip shows which project the session is bound to; backend-managed directory picker for switching between repos without losing context.
  • BE reliability pass — WAL-mode SQLite for the per- session stores, idle eviction so a long-lived BE releases memory once a session goes dormant, and a slow-client back-pressure fix so a single wedged view never stalls the other sessions' streams.
  • First-launch onboarding polish — clearer progress during the uv/Python bootstrap, IDE proxy is now propagated to the backend, disk-space precheck.

0.1.0

  • Zero-touch install — plugin bootstraps uv + Python + ignite-ember on first launch instead of requiring a manual pip install.
  • Editor integration — right-click selection / project view → "Add to Ember chat"; ⌘E to focus the chat panel.
  • Edits via the IDE — backend writes now route through WriteCommandAction so Local History records the change and editor undo works.
  • Indexed code search — paste handler uses PyCharm's trigram index for near-instant lookups instead of the rg subprocess.
  • IDE theme follows the chat panel (and vice-versa).

action.description: Send the current editor selection to the Ember chat composer
Attach this file (or selected files) to the Ember chat composer
Stop the Ember Python backend and start a fresh one
Wipe the managed Python cache and bootstrap from scratch
Dump plugin, CLI, and env information for bug reports
Switch between in-process JCEF (120 Hz) and out-of-process JCEF (60 Hz, sandboxed)
Show or hide the live FPS overlay in the igni panel
